projects.title: NEO BANKING
projects.description: A brutalist approach to modern banking interfaces. Disruptive finance solutions with aggressive styling.
projects.problem: Traditional banking apps are boring and safe. They lack personality.
projects.solution: Built a high-contrast, zero-apology interface that challenges the status quo.
projects.result: Increased user engagement by 300% due to sheer shock value.

projects.title: CYBER PUNK CMS
projects.description: Content Management System that screams back at you. Dark mode by default, blinding green accents.
projects.problem: CMS platforms look like Microsoft Word from 1998.
projects.solution: Injected raw terminal energy into a web interface.
projects.result: Developers forced non-tech users to use the terminal.

projects.title: CONCRETE E-COMMERCE
projects.description: Selling digital air with a tangible brutalist storefront.
projects.solution: Removed all rounded corners and added 4px solid black borders.

projects.title: RAW DATA VIZ
projects.description: Data visualization that doesn't hold your hand. Just the raw, unadulterated numbers.
projects.solution: Bar charts with razor-sharp edges and glitch animations.
projects.result: Data analysts wear sunglasses while working.

projects.title: NO SIGNAL SOCIAL
projects.description: An anti-social network where messages delete themselves.
projects.problem: Too much history on social media.
projects.solution: Every post is fleeting, visually aggressive, and noisy.

projects.title: GLITCH MEDIA
projects.description: A video streaming service where buffering is a feature, not a bug.
projects.solution: Intentional VHS artifacts and glitch effects.

projects.title: TERMINAL PORTFOLIO
projects.description: My old portfolio which was literally just a pseudo-terminal.
projects.solution: Command line interface on the web.
projects.result: Recruiters were too confused to hire me.

projects.title: AUDIO BRUTE
projects.description: A music player for industrial noise and harsh frequencies.

projects.title: GRID LOCK
projects.description: A puzzle game built entirely on CSS Grids crossing streams.
projects.solution: No images, just raw CSS borders.

projects.title: VOID ANALYTICS
projects.description: Analytics dashboard that highlights what you're doing wrong.
projects.solution: Only shows drop-off rates and lost revenue in bold red.

experience.description: Spearheaded the brutalist redesign of their core SaaS product. Removed 4000 lines of CSS rounding corners.
Built aggressive APIs that responded under 10ms. Implemented the 'No Apologies' error handler.
Maintained legacy spaghetti code and learned the true meaning of pain.

blogs.title: DEATH TO THE BORDER RADIUS
blogs.excerpt: Why softness in web design is a symptom of a weak society.
blogs.content: Look around you. Everything is soft. Rounded corners, drop shadows, gentle gradients. But life isn't soft. Life is sharp, jagged, and unforgiving. So why should our digital experiences be any different? In this manifesto, I argue for the complete annihilation of `border-radius` from all CSS files.

blogs.title: WHY I USE CAPS LOCK EXCLUSIVELY
blogs.content: WHEN YOU WRITE IN LOWERCASE, IT FEELS LIKE YOU'RE WHISPERING. I DON'T WANT TO WHISPER ON THE INTERNET. I WANT TO SHOUT. I WANT EVERY WORD I TYPE TO FEEL LIKE A BRICK THROWN THROUGH A VIRTUAL WINDOW. CAPS LOCK IS NOT JUST A KEY; IT'S A LIFESTYLE STATEMENT.

blogs.title: THE BEAUTY OF UGLY WEBSITES
blogs.excerpt: Aesthetics are a lie designed to sell you templates.
blogs.content: Beautiful sites are forgotten. Ugly sites are remembered. The friction, the cognitive load, the sheer audacity of an interface that refuses to pander to your sensibilities - this is where true digital art is born. Stop making things pretty. Start making things memorable.

blogs.title: EMBRACING THE RAW HTML
blogs.content: Sometimes I wonder if we should all just go back to plain HTML documents. Black text on a white background. Blue links. Active links turn red. Visited links turn purple. Why overcomplicate it?

blogs.title: MY HATRED FOR TAILWIND
blogs.excerpt: Utility classes are ruining our minds.
blogs.content: Writing 40 classes on a single div is not web development; it's masochism. Write plain CSS. Own your styles. Stop copying and pasting strings of gibberish. Be bold. Write your own aggressive global styles.

testimonials.quote: THE MOST INTENSE INTERFACE I'VE EVER USED. I FEEL LIKE I'M HACKING THE MAINFRAME ENROLLING IN HEALTHCARE.
testimonials.author: JOHN DOE, CEO OF TECH

testimonials.quote: HE REMOVED ALL THE ROUNDED CORNERS FROM OUR APP AND OUR CONVERSION RATE DOUBLED OUT OF PURE FEAR.
testimonials.author: JANE SMITH, PRODUCT MANAGER

testimonials.quote: IT'S UGLY. IT'S LOUD. IT WORKS PERFECTLY.
testimonials.author: ALICE ALICE, DESIGNER
